steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Express each concentration as a decimal: 4% = 0.04, 7% = 0.07, 5% = 0.05.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Compute the salt contributed by each solution: $x$ liters at 4% gives $0.04x$ liters of salt; $y$ liters at 7% gives $0.07y$ liters of salt.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: The combined volume is $x + y$ liters at 5%, so the salt in the mixture is $0.05(x+y)$. Setting total salt in equal to total salt out: $0.04x + 0.07y = 0.05(x+y)$.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
Evaluate every choice:
  • A: $0.04x + 0.07y = 5(x+y)$ — uses 5 instead of 0.05 on the right side, making the concentration 500%. Eliminated.
  • B: $0.04x + 0.07y = 0.05(x+y)$ — correctly balances salt in decimal form. That confirms B is correct.
  • C: $0.4x + 0.7y = 5(x+y)$ — scales both sides up by a factor of 10 on the left but 100 on the right. Inconsistent. Eliminated.
  • D: $0.4x + 0.7y = 0.5(x+y)$ — represents 40%, 70%, and 50% concentrations, not the given values. Eliminated.
